report_text: During sonar bench validation, channel 3 showed intermittent dropout after approximately 15 minutes of warmup. ADC saturation occurred twice during the same run. Restarting the acquisition controller temporarily restored normal readings.

target.suspected_cause: Possible thermal instability in the channel 3 analog signal path or associated front-end circuitry.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the channel 3 analog path, verify supply rails during thermal soak, and repeat the test with channel-level voltage logging.

report_text: The propulsion controller MCU reset three times during a commanded ramp from 20 to 70 percent thrust. The reset was preceded by watchdog events and a burst of high-rate status telemetry. Motor current stayed below the configured hard limit.

target.suspected_cause: Probable watchdog timeout caused by firmware scheduling pressure during simultaneous thrust control and telemetry activity.
target.recommended_action: Capture task timing and watchdog diagnostics, reduce telemetry rate during ramp tests, and reproduce with firmware trace instrumentation enabled.

report_text: After a 24-hour saltwater chamber test, the depth sensor reported a persistent positive offset of 0.42 meters at atmospheric pressure. The offset remained after power cycling but improved after running the calibration routine.

symptoms: positive depth offset at atmospheric pressure
target.suspected_cause: Calibration drift or environmental influence on the pressure-sensor reference after saltwater exposure.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the pressure port and reference path, perform a controlled calibration check, and compare results against a known-good sensor.

report_text: The telemetry link dropped from a typical 96 percent delivery rate to 61 percent during an offshore range test. RSSI varied sharply while vehicle orientation changed. Command acknowledgements were delayed but no commands were lost.

target.suspected_cause: Likely orientation-dependent antenna, connector, or propagation issue affecting link margin.
target.recommended_action: Inspect antenna and connector integrity, log orientation with RSSI, and repeat the range test using a fixed reference orientation.

report_text: During cold-start testing, the 12-volt bus dropped to 9.7 volts when the payload computer and illumination circuit started together. The computer rebooted once. Battery state of charge was 72 percent and recovered to normal voltage after the load stabilized.

target.suspected_cause: Transient inrush demand exceeded available cold-start power margin or the distribution path has excessive resistance.
target.recommended_action: Measure inrush current and voltage drop across the battery, fuse, connectors, and distribution wiring; add staged startup or soft-start control if needed.

report_text: The event-ingest service recorded duplicate fault reports after the upstream client retried a request during a network timeout. Two records had the same device identifier, timestamp, and payload but different database primary keys.

target.suspected_cause: Missing or ineffective idempotency handling at the API or persistence boundary.
target.recommended_action: Add an idempotency key or deterministic event identifier, enforce uniqueness at the persistence layer, and add retry-focused integration tests.

report_text: Post-dive inspection found a small quantity of water inside the electronics enclosure near the forward cable penetrator. Internal humidity rose during the dive and the leak indicator changed state, but primary electronics remained operational.

target.suspected_cause: Possible seal, torque, cable-jacket, or penetrator-interface failure allowing water ingress.
target.recommended_action: Remove the vehicle from service, inspect and pressure-test the penetrator assembly, replace compromised seals, and verify enclosure integrity before redeployment.

report_text: The operator reported that a yellow warning LED flashed twice during a transit run. No diagnostic code was stored, vehicle performance appeared normal, and the event could not be reproduced during a short bench test.

target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to identify the affected subsystem or fault mechanism.
target.recommended_action: Enable persistent fault-event logging, capture timestamped telemetry during the next transit run, and escalate if the indication recurs.

report_text: The low-light camera stream developed repeated horizontal artifacts after the vehicle switched from standby to active capture mode. Frames remained available, but artifact frequency increased with cable movement near the imaging module.

target.suspected_cause: Probable intermittent cable, connector, grounding, or high-speed data-link integrity issue.
target.recommended_action: Inspect and strain-relieve the imaging cable, reseat connectors, check grounding continuity, and repeat capture while monitoring link errors.

report_text: A new acquisition-profile deployment produced measurements approximately 18 percent lower than the prior validated run. Hardware self-tests passed, but the loaded configuration showed a gain value different from the approved calibration sheet.

symptoms: configured gain differs from approved calibration
target.suspected_cause: Incorrect gain or calibration configuration was deployed to the acquisition system.
target.recommended_action: Block further collection with the affected profile, restore the approved calibration configuration, and add configuration identity checks to deployment validation.

report_text: During a harness-flex test, the temperature channel jumped between 4.1 C and 27.8 C while the reference thermometer remained stable. The readings returned to normal whenever the sensor cable was held stationary.

symptoms: temperature readings spike during cable flex
readings normalize when harness is stationary
target.suspected_cause: Intermittent harness, connector, or conductor fault in the temperature-sensor signal path.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the sensor harness and connector, perform continuity testing while flexing the cable, and replace any damaged segment before redeployment.

report_text: ADC channel 5 developed periodic noise bursts whenever the hydraulic pump switched on. Adjacent channels stayed within normal variance, and the noise disappeared when the pump power circuit was disconnected.

symptoms: noise coincides with hydraulic pump activation
target.suspected_cause: Electrical interference, grounding issue, or supply coupling between the pump circuit and channel 5 analog path.
target.recommended_action: Measure analog-ground and supply noise during pump switching, inspect shielding and grounding, and repeat the test with the pump circuit isolated.

report_text: The imaging module produced brief black frames when the vehicle crossed a vibration threshold on the bench shaker. Video resumed immediately, but the fault could be triggered by moving the connector strain-relief near the camera housing.

symptoms: connector strain-relief movement triggers the issue
target.suspected_cause: Intermittent camera data-link connector, cable, or strain-relief failure.
target.recommended_action: Inspect and reseat the imaging connector, verify strain relief, perform vibration testing with link-error monitoring, and replace the cable assembly if the fault recurs.

report_text: After a pressure-cycle test, water was found inside the aft service compartment near the hatch flange. The moisture indicator was active and insulation resistance on one accessory circuit had decreased from its pre-test value.

symptoms: reduced insulation resistance after pressure cycle
target.suspected_cause: Likely hatch-seal, flange, fastener-torque, or enclosure-interface failure allowing water ingress.
target.recommended_action: Remove the assembly from service, inspect the hatch seal and flange surfaces, pressure-test the compartment, replace compromised seals, and verify electrical insulation before reuse.

report_text: A slow oil seep developed around the pressure-compensation bladder fitting after repeated thermal cycling. No water was detected and internal pressure remained stable, but the fitting exterior was visibly wet after each cold soak.

symptoms: fitting exterior remains wet after cold soak
target.suspected_cause: Thermal cycling may have degraded the fitting seal, clamp preload, or mating-surface integrity.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the fitting and seal stack, verify clamp torque, replace suspect sealing components, and repeat thermal-cycle testing before deployment.

report_text: A vibration survey found that the forward sensor bracket developed increasing play after three hours of shaker exposure. The sensor still reported data, but the mounting fasteners required retightening after the test.

target.suspected_cause: Insufficient fastener retention or mount stiffness under the tested vibration profile.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the bracket and fasteners, add appropriate locking features or preload control, and repeat the vibration survey with displacement monitoring.

report_text: An amber status indicator appeared for approximately two seconds during a return transit. No fault code was stored, no telemetry anomaly was recorded, and the indicator could not be reproduced during a forty-minute bench run.

target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to identify a subsystem or fault mechanism.
target.recommended_action: Enable persistent event logging, capture timestamped telemetry during future transits, and escalate if the indicator recurs or correlates with another anomaly.

report_text: One propulsion-current sample exceeded the expected envelope by 14 percent during a six-hour endurance test. All later samples were normal, the motor temperature stayed nominal, and no controller diagnostic was recorded.

target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to distinguish measurement noise, transient loading, or an emerging propulsion issue.
target.recommended_action: Retain the raw trace, increase current-sampling resolution for the next endurance run, and require review if the excursion repeats or trends upward.

report_text: An operator reported hearing an intermittent relay-like click from the payload bay during startup. The sound was not captured in logs, no associated voltage change was found, and a later startup sequence was normal.

target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to identify the source or determine whether the sound represents a fault.
target.recommended_action: Add synchronized startup audio or event capture if practical, inspect relay and contactor assemblies during scheduled maintenance, and monitor for recurrence.

report_text: The regulated 5-volt rail fell to 4.42 volts when the actuator bank and navigation computer initialized together. The navigation computer did not reboot, but its USB sensor disconnected and re-enumerated twice.

target.suspected_cause: Startup inrush or distribution resistance is reducing available 5-volt power margin.
target.recommended_action: Measure startup current and voltage drop across the regulator and distribution path, sequence high-inrush loads, and verify regulator transient response.

report_text: After cold storage, one battery cell group reached the low-voltage warning threshold twelve minutes before the other groups during a fixed-load discharge test. Pack voltage remained above shutdown level, but the imbalance repeated on a second run.

symptoms: one cell group reaches warning threshold early
imbalance repeats on second discharge test
target.suspected_cause: Possible cell imbalance, reduced cold-temperature capacity, or inaccurate cell-group measurement.
target.recommended_action: Perform a controlled balance and capacity check, verify cell-sense wiring, and remove the pack from mission use if the imbalance exceeds the approved limit.

report_text: The command processor stopped accepting new mission commands after sustained message traffic. Existing control loops continued running, but the command queue depth remained fixed and the watchdog reset the controller after its timeout period.

symptoms: new mission commands stop being accepted
watchdog reset after sustained message traffic
target.suspected_cause: Probable command-queue deadlock, task starvation, or synchronization failure under sustained traffic.
target.recommended_action: Capture task and queue diagnostics, reproduce under controlled load, inspect synchronization paths, and add a queue-progress watchdog metric.

report_text: Following a normal reboot, the controller loaded a fallback configuration even though the active profile checksum matched the stored value. The system remained operable, but one payload option reverted to its default state.

target.suspected_cause: Firmware configuration-loading logic may be incorrectly rejecting or partially applying a valid stored profile.
target.recommended_action: Collect boot diagnostics, add explicit configuration-load reason codes, reproduce with the affected profile, and inspect the fallback-selection logic.

report_text: During a range test, telemetry packet delivery declined from 94 percent to 68 percent when the vehicle rotated broadside to the shore station. RSSI dropped at the same time, while onboard sensor data remained internally consistent.

symptoms: onboard sensor data remains internally consistent
target.suspected_cause: Orientation-dependent RF propagation, antenna pattern, connector, or installation issue is reducing link margin.
target.recommended_action: Inspect antenna installation and connectors, correlate orientation with RSSI, and repeat the range test using a controlled antenna orientation reference.

report_text: The RS-485 sensor bus accumulated CRC errors after the tether was routed alongside the motor power cable. Communication recovered when the tether was separated from the power bundle, and no sensor faults were reported locally.

symptoms: errors appear when tether is near motor power cable
target.suspected_cause: Electromagnetic interference or inadequate shielding is degrading the differential communications bus.
target.recommended_action: Separate the bus from high-current wiring, inspect shield termination and grounding, verify termination resistance, and repeat testing under motor load.

report_text: After a firmware rollout, the navigation solution showed a persistent heading offset despite passing the standard sensor self-test. The loaded configuration referenced an older IMU bias-table version than the currently approved release.

symptoms: persistent heading offset after firmware rollout
target.suspected_cause: An outdated IMU bias configuration was deployed with the new firmware release.
target.recommended_action: Block mission use with the affected configuration, restore the approved bias table, and add configuration-version verification to release validation.

report_text: A flow sensor reported values consistently 11 percent above the laboratory reference after a maintenance replacement. The physical flow path was unobstructed, and the discrepancy remained stable across three test points.

target.suspected_cause: Incorrect scale factor, calibration coefficient, or replacement-sensor configuration.
target.recommended_action: Verify the installed sensor identity and calibration coefficients, compare against the approved configuration, and recalibrate against a traceable reference.

report_text: The event-processing service displayed fault reports out of chronological order when two field nodes reconnected after an outage. Server receipt timestamps were monotonic, but device timestamps differed by several minutes between nodes.

symptoms: fault reports displayed out of chronological order
target.suspected_cause: Clock skew or event-ordering logic is causing records from different devices to be sorted incorrectly.
target.recommended_action: Record both device and receipt timestamps, define an ordering policy for delayed events, add clock-skew monitoring, and create reconnection-order integration tests.

report_text: During thermal-soak testing, the conductivity sensor output drifted only when the instrument ground strap was loosened. The reference supply remained stable, and the readings returned to normal after the ground strap was refastened.

symptoms: conductivity output drifts during thermal soak
drift appears when ground strap is loose
readings normalize after ground strap is refastened
target.suspected_cause: Ground-reference instability or an intermittent grounding connection is affecting the sensor analog signal path.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the ground strap and bonding surfaces, measure ground potential during thermal soak, and repeat the test after correcting the connection.

report_text: During a surface range test, command acknowledgements became delayed and telemetry packet loss increased when the shore-station antenna was partially obstructed by a service vehicle. Internal onboard logs remained complete and sensor values were stable.

target.suspected_cause: Reduced RF link margin caused by antenna obstruction or degraded line of sight between the shore station and vehicle.
target.recommended_action: Restore a clear antenna path, inspect antenna placement and connectors, and repeat the range test while recording RSSI and packet-delivery metrics.

report_text: A single pressure reading briefly exceeded the expected operating envelope during a long endurance run. The next samples returned to normal, the independent reference sensor did not show the same event, and no diagnostic code was recorded.

symptoms: single pressure reading exceeds expected envelope
independent reference does not confirm event
target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to determine whether the event was sensor noise, transient process variation, or an emerging pressure-system fault.
target.recommended_action: Retain the raw trace, increase sampling and cross-check logging on the next endurance run, and escalate if the event repeats or correlates with another anomaly.

report_text: Post-test inspection found a hairline crack in a non-pressure-bearing external fairing near a mounting fastener. No water ingress was detected, the enclosure remained dry, and the crack did not extend into the structural housing.

symptoms: crack does not reach structural housing
target.suspected_cause: Localized stress concentration, fastener preload, or vibration loading may have caused the fairing crack.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the mounting geometry and fastener preload, repair or replace the fairing, and verify that vibration loading does not propagate the crack.

report_text: During payload startup, the 24-volt bus remained nominal but the local 12-volt converter output dipped below 10 volts for approximately 180 milliseconds. The payload computer restarted once and the fault repeated whenever the illumination load was enabled at the same time.

symptoms: fault repeats when illumination load starts simultaneously
target.suspected_cause: Converter transient response, downstream inrush demand, or local distribution resistance is insufficient for simultaneous payload startup.
target.recommended_action: Measure converter input and output during startup, characterize illumination inrush current, inspect local power wiring, and sequence the loads or add soft-start control.

report_text: After an over-the-air firmware update, the controller booted normally but retained a legacy actuator-limit value instead of the new release default. The configuration file was present, and the update log reported no write error.

symptoms: legacy actuator-limit value remains after firmware update
update log reports no write error
target.suspected_cause: Firmware migration or configuration-default logic failed to update an existing persisted value.
target.recommended_action: Reproduce the update on a controlled device, inspect migration and configuration precedence logic, add post-update configuration assertions, and block release until the correct value is loaded.

report_text: The telemetry aggregation service posted the same deployment summary twice after a worker restart. The second summary had the same source-event identifiers but a different processing timestamp, and downstream dashboards counted both records.

symptoms: duplicate deployment summaries after worker restart
target.suspected_cause: Worker restart recovery is replaying already-processed events without idempotent aggregation or deduplication.
target.recommended_action: Use deterministic source-event keys, enforce idempotent writes, record worker checkpoints, and add restart-replay integration tests.

report_text: The dissolved-oxygen sensor signal became noisy only when the external lamp driver entered pulse-width modulation mode. The sensor electronics remained powered continuously, and the noise spectrum matched the lamp switching frequency.

symptoms: sensor signal becomes noisy during lamp PWM operation
noise spectrum matches lamp switching frequency
target.suspected_cause: Switching interference is coupling from the lamp driver into the sensor analog path through grounding, supply, shielding, or cable routing.
target.recommended_action: Measure coupling on the sensor input and supply rails, improve separation and shielding, inspect grounding, and repeat the test with the lamp driver isolated.

report_text: The acoustic modem completed local self-tests but failed to receive acknowledgements from the surface gateway after the gateway antenna cable was replaced. Transmit power readings were normal, and onboard logs showed repeated retry attempts with no returned packet.

symptoms: repeated retries with no returned packet
target.suspected_cause: Surface-gateway antenna cable, connector, installation, or link-path failure is preventing successful two-way communications.
target.recommended_action: Inspect the replacement cable and connectors, verify gateway receive path and antenna installation, test with a known-good cable, and capture bidirectional link diagnostics.

report_text: A newly deployed acquisition profile sampled a vibration channel at 2 kilohertz instead of the approved 8 kilohertz rate. Hardware diagnostics passed, but the resulting spectra no longer captured the expected high-frequency features.

symptoms: sampling rate is lower than approved value
target.suspected_cause: Incorrect acquisition-profile parameter was deployed for the vibration channel.
target.recommended_action: Suspend collection with the affected profile, restore the approved sampling-rate configuration, and add profile identity and parameter checks before deployment.

report_text: After repeated deck handling, the protective boot around a noncritical external connector tore near its clamp band. The connector remained dry during inspection and continuity checks passed, but the boot no longer provided complete environmental coverage.

symptoms: protective boot torn near clamp band
target.suspected_cause: Mechanical abrasion, clamp-band stress, or repeated handling has damaged the connector protective boot.
target.recommended_action: Replace the damaged boot, inspect clamp-band fit and cable routing, and verify connector sealing before exposure to wet conditions.

report_text: During a routine mission, one operator observed a brief change in propulsion sound near the end of the run. Motor current, temperature, vibration telemetry, and post-run inspection were all normal, and the sound could not be reproduced on a follow-up test.

symptoms: event not reproduced on follow-up test
target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to identify whether the observed sound represented a transient load change, environmental contact, or propulsion-system fault.
target.recommended_action: Retain synchronized audio and telemetry on future missions, inspect propulsion hardware during scheduled maintenance, and escalate if the sound recurs with supporting telemetry.

report_text: A forward-looking camera developed intermittent colored streaks whenever its cable bundle was pressed against the vehicle frame. The video stream stayed connected, and the artifacts disappeared when the bundle was repositioned away from the frame.

symptoms: artifacts occur when cable bundle contacts frame
target.suspected_cause: Mechanical stress, grounding, shielding, or connector integrity issue in the camera data-link path.
target.recommended_action: Inspect cable routing and strain relief, verify connector retention and shielding continuity, isolate the bundle from the frame, and repeat vibration testing with video-error monitoring.

report_text: After loading a new navigation software build, altitude estimates were consistently 6 percent low while the pressure sensor self-test passed. Release records showed that the deployed package referenced an older depth-to-altitude conversion table.

target.suspected_cause: An outdated conversion-table configuration was packaged or selected during deployment.
target.recommended_action: Block use of the affected navigation package, deploy the approved conversion table, and add configuration-version verification to the release process.

report_text: Following a dive, the leak detector reported moisture inside the battery compartment and insulation resistance on the main battery leads was below the pre-dive measurement. The outer housing showed no visible damage, but water was present beneath the battery tray.

symptoms: reduced insulation resistance on battery leads
target.suspected_cause: A battery-compartment seal, penetrator, or internal interface may be allowing water ingress despite an intact outer housing.
target.recommended_action: Remove the vehicle from service, isolate battery power, inspect and pressure-test the battery compartment and penetrators, replace compromised seals, and verify insulation resistance before reuse.

report_text: During a coastal survey, the vehicle continued recording internally but received no new mission updates for six minutes. RSSI at the support station fell below the normal threshold during that interval, and command acknowledgements resumed after the vehicle changed heading.

symptoms: command acknowledgements resume after heading change
target.suspected_cause: Orientation-dependent RF link-margin loss is interrupting command and telemetry traffic while onboard systems remain operational.
target.recommended_action: Inspect antenna orientation and installation, correlate heading with RSSI and delivery rate, repeat the survey with controlled headings, and improve link margin before operational use.

report_text: A technician observed one brief flicker of the status display during pre-launch checkout. No code was recorded, all measurements remained normal, and the display behaved normally during three later power cycles.

target.suspected_cause: Insufficient evidence to determine whether the event was a display transient, power disturbance, or an emerging subsystem fault.
target.recommended_action: Enable persistent display and power-event logging, inspect connectors during routine maintenance, and escalate if the event recurs or correlates with another anomaly.

report_text: After a temporary network outage, the incident dashboard showed several older device faults after newer faults from the same device. Device sequence numbers were intact, but the ingestion service displayed records in server-arrival order rather than source-event order.

symptoms: older device faults appear after newer faults
target.suspected_cause: The ingestion or presentation layer is ordering delayed events by receipt time instead of source sequence or source-event time.
target.recommended_action: Define and implement source-aware ordering, retain receipt and source timestamps, add delayed-delivery integration tests, and expose ordering provenance in the dashboard.
